doing some massages it goes away.

Sensory: Light touch, pinprick, cold, position & vibration sensation is intact throughout &

draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: Patient is homebound due to peripheral neuropathy and lumbar radiculopathy.

draftNarratives.label: Medical Necessity
draftNarratives.draft: Medical necessity for home health services is due to peripheral neuropathy and lumbar radiculopathy.

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: Patient Gloria Allen, a 74-year-old female, presents with peripheral neuropathy, lumbar radiculopathy, and carpal tunnel syndrome. She is homebound due to her conditions and requires occupational therapy for hand stiffness and lumbar radiculopathy. She has a history of back pain, cervical and lumbar spondylosis, and menopausal status. Patient is active, running and doing yoga and weights.

draftNarratives.label: Skilled Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Occupational therapy for hand fingers stiffness (fingers getting stuck) and lumbar radiculopathy to RLE.

draftNarratives.label: Plan For Next Visit
draftNarratives.draft: Occupational therapy for hand fingers stiffness (fingers getting stuck) and lumbar radiculopathy to RLE.

draftNarratives.label: Care Plan Problems / Goals / Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Problems: Peripheral neuropathy, lumbar radiculopathy, carpal tunnel syndrome. Goals: Manage symptoms, improve function. Interventions: Occupational therapy for hand stiffness and lumbar radiculopathy.

draftNarratives.label: Patient / Caregiver Goals
draftNarratives.draft: Patient goals include managing symptoms of peripheral neuropathy and lumbar radiculopathy, and improving hand function. Caregiver goals include supporting patient's therapy and daily activities.
